patch.value:
## Defect
Observed live (2026-07-10) while filing pm-kvev: `--dep "related:pm-x26a"` was accepted, prefix-normalized to dependency id `pm-related:pm-x26a`, and stored as a dangling edge (`pm deps` reports missing: true, missing_count: 1). No warning at write time. The documented grammar is `id=<id>,kind=<kind>[,author=...,created_at=...,source_kind=...]`.

## Why this hurts agents
Write-time silence violates the never-block-never-corrupt principle: the mutation looks successful, the graph silently degrades, and the defect only surfaces later via pm deps/validate on a different item. Repairing requires knowing the --replace-deps + --dep combination (--clear-deps + --dep is rejected as conflicting).

## Fix outline
In the shared dependency-flag parser used by create/update: (1) values containing '=' or ',' that fail CSV parsing -> hard error with the grammar hint; (2) colon-containing bare values -> same error (colon is never legal in an item id); (3) optional existence check on the normalized id emitting a written-with-warning envelope consistent with strict parent validation.

## Duplicate evidence
Searches (dep syntax invalid id validation / dependency missing node create / dangling dependency dep flag shorthand) return no open owner; pm-dlfq (GH-98, closed) fixed kind parsing not id validation; pm-orrl (closed) covered --blocked-by free text; pm-q6gx owns GH-484 blocked-by scalar overwrite only.
pm create/update --dep silently normalizes malformed shorthand into dangling dependency ids (related:pm-x26a -> pm-related:pm-x26a)
Malformed --dep values that miss the documented id=,kind= CSV form are treated as bare ids and prefix-normalized into nonexistent nodes instead of erroring with the expected syntax.

Malformed --dep values containing '=',':' or ',' but not matching the documented CSV grammar fail fast with the expected id=<id>,kind=<kind> hint; bare-id values that do not resolve to an existing item after prefix normalization warn or error instead of writing a dangling edge; regression tests cover create and update paths
pm create issue t --ac a --dep related:pm-x26a --json; pm deps <new-id> shows dependency id pm-related:pm-x26a with missing: true
